added.name: Innovation: Names What's New AND What It Replaces
added.description: The innovation section must identify the current standard approach and explain specifically why it falls short before claiming what is new. Innovation is relative — reviewers cannot appreciate what's novel unless they understand what it replaces. The section should make the old approach feel inadequate and the new approach feel like the only credible path forward.

reweighted.name: Strategic Framing of Preliminary Work & Personnel
Earned Authority — Claims Grounded, Not Asserted

reworded.name: Narrative Arc & 'Why Now' Urgency
reworded.old: The writing builds a story about why this work matters right now — not a list of facts or statistics. Each section should create tension around a gap, establish what has changed to make the question answerable, and make the reader feel urgency rather than just understand it. Closings must deliver resolution with weight, not announce conclusions.
reworded.new: The significance section tells a story — not a literature review. Each sentence should make the gap feel more urgent than the last, until the proposed work feels like the only logical next step. The reader should feel the cost of inaction, not just understand that a gap exists.

reworded.name: Standalone-Yet-Connected Aim Architecture
reworded.old: Each aim produces something independently valuable — a deliverable, a resolved question, or a contribution the field gains regardless of other aims. At the same time, the aim naturally points forward so the reader senses a next chapter without feeling the aim is merely setup or contingent on what follows.
reworded.new: Each aim produces something independently valuable — a deliverable, a resolved question, or a contribution the field gains regardless of other aims. At the same time, completing all aims together produces something greater than the sum of parts. Reviewers penalize both disconnected aims and fragile dependency chains — this balance is the target.

reworded.name: Strategic Framing of Preliminary Work & Personnel
reworded.old: Pilot data is positioned as a feasibility gate that reveals the next necessary question — not as a highlight reel of accomplishments. Personnel are framed in terms of scientific enablement (why this question is tractable because of them) rather than credential listing.
reworded.new: Pilot data is positioned as a feasibility gate — proof that the team can do this work and that the next question is now answerable. It must not read as a highlight reel of accomplishments. The message is 'we can do this,' not 'we already did this.' Personnel descriptions belong in the scientific argument, not a credential list.

reworded.name: Earned Authority — Claims Grounded, Not Asserted
reworded.old: Every novelty claim, priority claim, or capability assertion is substantiated with specific technical, methodological, or empirical detail. A skeptical reviewer should finish each claim thinking 'I see why' rather than being asked to take it on faith. Descriptions of existing tools or data must not accidentally make the scientific challenge seem trivial or already solved.
reworded.new: Every novelty claim, priority claim, or capability assertion is substantiated with specific technical, methodological, or empirical detail. A skeptical reviewer should finish each claim thinking 'I see why' rather than being asked to take it on faith. Risks must be named — overpromising without acknowledging uncertainty is a dealbreaker.

dimensions_changed.name: Narrative Arc & 'Why Now' Urgency
added_dims: By the end of the significance section, the proposed work feels like the obvious and necessary next step — not one option among many
Paragraph and section endings deliver earned resolution — they assert rather than announce, and their emotional register matches the strongest moments in the passage
Each sentence in the significance section adds pressure — the gap feels more urgent as the section progresses, not just restated
removed_dims: A clear 'why now' argument identifies what has changed (technically, conceptually, or methodologically) to make this work timely

Paragraph and section endings deliver earned resolution — they assert rather than announce, are declarative rather than interrogative, and their emotional register matches the strongest moments in the passage

dimensions_changed.name: Standalone-Yet-Connected Aim Architecture
added_dims: When all aims are read together, a reviewer can see that their combination produces a scientific contribution beyond what any single aim delivers alone
Each aim is focused enough to be achievable — it does not try to answer multiple major questions or serve as a proposal within a proposal

dimensions_changed.name: Cross-Disciplinary Accessibility
added_dims: Technical specifics are introduced as evidence within the narrative argument, not as standalone inventories that break the story's momentum
removed_dims: Technical specifics (sensor types, measurement parameters, method details) are introduced as evidence within the narrative argument, not as standalone inventories that break the story's momentum

dimensions_changed.name: Strategic Framing of Preliminary Work & Personnel
added_dims: Key personnel are introduced through the scientific or methodological condition they satisfy — why this team makes this question tractable
The pilot framing makes clear what the funded work will do that the pilot cannot — the 'already done' problem is actively avoided
Every claim about resources, personnel allocation, or timeline is explicitly connected to a specific scientific goal — reviewers can trace each logistical decision back to the science it enables
removed_dims: The pilot framing makes clear what the funded work will do that the pilot cannot, avoiding the 'already done' problem
Key personnel are introduced through the scientific or methodological condition they satisfy, not through qualifications alone

dimensions_changed.name: Earned Authority — Claims Grounded, Not Asserted
added_dims: Key methodological risks or challenges are named explicitly and reframed as the rationale for the work — not hidden, hedged, or buried
Technical capabilities are described in concrete terms rather than generic competence language
The writing uses active voice that names who is doing the work — passive constructions that obscure agency are avoided throughout
Descriptions of available tools, data, or existing work do not accidentally collapse the perceived difficulty of the scientific challenge
removed_dims: Descriptions of available tools, data, or existing work do not accidentally collapse the perceived difficulty of the scientific challenge — the gap should feel harder to close as context accumulates, not easier
Key methodological risks or challenges are named and reframed as the rationale for the work rather than hidden or hedged
Technical capabilities are described in concrete terms (mechanisms, specifications, design features) rather than generic competence language

reasoning: I made four main changes: (1) I sharpened the Narrative Arc criterion to explicitly describe the significance section's job as escalating urgency sentence-by-sentence until the proposed work feels inevitable — not just 'telling a story.' (2) I added a dedicated Innovation criterion that captures my core requirement: name what's new AND what it replaces, with the current approach explicitly described so the contrast lands. This was completely missing from the rubric. (3) I added a 'logistics trace to science' dimension under Preliminary Work to capture my strong preference that every resource or timeline claim connect to a specific scientific goal. (4) I added an active voice dimension under Earned Authority since passive voice is one of my dealbreakers and it wasn't represented anywhere. I also added a scope discipline dimension to Aim Architecture to flag the 'unfocused aims' dealbreaker more directly.

reviewed_changes.original_text: Answering a causal question about early microbial dysbiosis and neurodevelopment requires something that has not previously existed: paired, time-resolved biological and behavioral samples from children followed prospectively through the window when both systems are still in formation.
reviewed_changes.new_text: The causal question — whether early microbial dysbiosis shapes neurodevelopmental trajectories in ASD — has resisted answer not for lack of interest, but for lack of the right conditions arriving at once.
reviewed_changes.reason: Narrative Arc 'urgency_escalates' (new dimension): The original sentence front-loaded the solution (what's needed) before establishing why the question has failed. The revision names the failure first, building pressure before the solution arrives — so sentence two adds urgency rather than restating the opening.

reviewed_changes.original_text: one that cannot be reconstructed after the fact, because the window it was built to capture is closing
reviewed_changes.new_text: samples from children followed prospectively through the window when both gut and brain are still in formation, a window that is closing
reviewed_changes.reason: Earned Authority 'active_voice_ownership' (new dimension): 'the window it was built to capture is closing' buries the closing window in a relative clause about the cohort's construction. The revision makes 'a window that is closing' the active, felt fact — and names both systems (gut and brain) explicitly for precision and cross-disciplinary clarity.

reviewed_changes.original_text: to the point where they can finally deliver the resolution the causal question requires — not just pattern description, but mechanistic signal
reviewed_changes.new_text: to the point where they can deliver mechanistic signal — not just pattern description
reviewed_changes.reason: Narrative Arc 'urgency_escalates' (new dimension): 'finally deliver the resolution the causal question requires' is slightly circular and slows momentum. Tightening to 'deliver mechanistic signal — not just pattern description' sharpens the escalation and removes the self-referential framing.

reviewed_changes.original_text: the preclinical work has sharpened enough to give that signal a target: specific microbial metabolites, including
reviewed_changes.new_text: the preclinical work has now sharpened that signal to a specific target:
reviewed_changes.reason: Earned Authority 'active_voice_ownership' (new dimension) and Narrative Arc 'urgency_escalates' (new dimension): 'has sharpened enough to give that signal a target' is slightly passive in agency — the revision names the action more directly ('sharpened that signal to a specific target') and adds 'now' to mark the temporal arrival, increasing urgency pressure on this sentence.

reviewed_changes.original_text: None of these conditions existed simultaneously until now. Together, they are not a résumé — they are a runway.
reviewed_changes.new_text: Each of these conditions alone would be insufficient. Together, they make the question answerable for the first time — but only if we act now.
reviewed_changes.reason: Narrative Arc 'proposed_work_feels_inevitable' (new dimension): 'They are a runway' is evocative but leaves the next step open — runways lead somewhere, but don't specify where or that this is the only direction. The revision explicitly stakes the claim: answerable for the first time, but contingent on acting now. This tightens the inevitability of the proposal.

reviewed_changes.original_text: The children who will age out of the earliest, most neurologically plastic period of development will do so in
reviewed_changes.new_text: The children in our cohort will age out of the earliest, most neurologically plastic period of development within
reviewed_changes.reason: Earned Authority 'active_voice_ownership' (new dimension): The original construction is grammatically awkward (repeated 'will') and doesn't name the cohort as the subject. Adding 'in our cohort' grounds the claim in the team's specific asset, and 'within' is more precise than 'in' for a time range.

reviewed_changes.original_text: That is the cost of waiting.
reviewed_changes.new_text: That is the cost of waiting: not a delay in knowledge, but a permanent narrowing of what that knowledge can change.
reviewed_changes.reason: Narrative Arc 'urgency_escalates' and 'urgency_felt' (escalation dimension and existing dimension): The original sentence lands the phrase but doesn't expand it. The revision defines the cost concretely — not just a temporal delay but a permanent foreclosure of actionability — which makes the stakes feel irreversible rather than merely unfortunate.

reviewed_changes.original_text: This proposal exists because we are, for the first time, positioned not just to ask whether early microbial dysbiosis shapes neurodevelopmental trajectories in ASD, but to answer that question while the answer can still change what we do next.
reviewed_changes.new_text: No other team has this cohort. No other cohort has this window. This proposal is not a well-timed opportunity — it is the only path to an answer that still has the power to redirect care.
reviewed_changes.reason: Narrative Arc 'proposed_work_feels_inevitable' (new dimension) and 'closing_lands_with_weight' (existing dimension): The original closing is logically sound but reads as positioning ('we are positioned') rather than assertion. The revision makes three declarative claims that foreclose alternatives — no other team, no other cohort, not one option among many — culminating in a closing that asserts inevitability rather than announcing readiness.
reviewed_changes.user_feedback: I get what it's going for — the three short declarative sentences are punchy and I like the energy. But 'No other team has this cohort. No other cohort has this window.' reads as a little combative, almost daring a reviewer to push back. If a study section member knows of a comparable cohort, that opener immediately puts them on the defensive. The original 'we are positioned' framing was too soft, I agree, but I'd rather land the inevitability through the specificity of what we bring — something like naming the convergence of assets — than through exclusivity claims that could backfire. The closing should feel like a conclusion the reader arrives at, not a challenge we issue.
